slides.title: Digital Infrastructure Industry Research
slides.subtitle: Market structure, growth drivers, and investment implications

slides.title: Digital infrastructure remains investable, but returns are shifting toward power-secure and contracted assets
bullets: AI workloads are increasing demand for high-density capacity and reliable power access.
Capital intensity is rising as grid, cooling, and land constraints become more pronounced.
Contract quality and customer concentration will drive valuation dispersion.
Investors should separate platform scarcity value from project-level development risk.
slides.source: Source: Public industry sources; IBDeck analysis.

slides.title: Four structural themes should frame investment screening across the sector
rows: Grid capacity, PPA cost, interconnection queue
Rack density, cooling design, capex per MW
Land bank, utility relationships, development team

slides.title: Illustrative capital intensity rises as facilities shift toward AI-ready specifications
